# pexip_infinity_break_in_allow_list_address (Resource)

Manages a break-in allow list address with the Infinity service. Break-in allow list addresses define IP address ranges that are exempt from certain security restrictions, allowing specified networks to bypass break-in attempt detection for specific scenarios.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "pexip_infinity_break_in_allow_list_address" "example" {
  name    = "Corporate Office"
  address = "10.0.0.0"
  prefix  = 8
}
```


<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `address` (String) The IP address for this allow list entry.
- `name` (String) The name of the break-in allow list entry. Maximum length: 100 characters.
- `prefix` (Number) The network prefix length (CIDR notation). Valid range: 0-128 (supports both IPv4 and IPv6).

### Optional

- `allowlist_entry_type` (String) The entry type of this address range. Use 'user' for IP ranges containing trusted end-user workstations or hardware video endpoints; use 'proxy' for trusted reverse proxies. Valid values: user, proxy.
- `description` (String) Description of the break-in allow list entry. Maximum length: 500 characters.
- `ignore_incorrect_aliases` (Boolean) Whether to ignore incorrect alias attempts from this address range.
- `ignore_incorrect_pins` (Boolean) Whether to ignore incorrect PIN attempts from this address range.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Resource URI for the break-in allow list address in Infinity
- `resource_id` (Number) The resource integer identifier for the break-in allow list address in Infinity
